title: DNA parts catalog -- canonical part record
description: The canonical, authored representation of one part. The .gb file is generated from this by tools/build_gb.py; prose lives in the sibling <slug>.md.

locus_annotations.description: LOCUS-line annotations carried for a faithful .gb (topology, division, date).

features.description: GenBank features in file order; the first parent-less feature is the main part.

type.description: GenBank/INSDC feature key (the shared vocabulary with the seqmake app), plus the de-facto synbio extension protein_domain. The Sequence Ontology accession is a derived /db_xref, mapped from this key by tools/so_terms.py. Deprecated INSDC signal keys (-35_signal/-10_signal) are expressed as regulatory + /regulatory_class; the display-only 'resistance' pseudo-type is not a feature key.

provenance.description: How this record was produced (free-form; e.g. migrated_from, asserting agent/model).

functional_claims.description: Prose-derived functional knowledge (inducer, dynamic range, host range, ...). Each claim is self-describing: its source, extraction provenance, and the orthogonal trust axes (confidence, usefulness, claim type) plus its verification lifecycle (analysis_status / cross_checked) travel with it so the knowledge base can self-correct (a nanopublication shape, kept mutable in git). See proposals/cross-check/CLAIM-MODEL.md.

id.description: stable id unique within the part

type.description: claim kind, e.g. inducer / repression_dynamic_range / induction_dynamic_range / strength_class / host_range / mechanism

ontology.description: verified ontology mappings (ChEBI/OM/NCBI-taxon); a key may be null when not yet mapped (no guessed IRIs).

source.description: the literature the claim is drawn from, as granular as possible: prefer a verbatim quote and/or a figure/table/page locator over a bare PMID.

quote_source.description: where the quote was taken from: 'primary' (the paper) or 'catalog-doc' (the curated .md)

provenance.description: how the claim was produced: method, asserting agent/model, source doc, timestamp

confidence.description: how well the cited primary source supports the claim

usefulness.description: how much the claim helps a designer choose/operate the part (orthogonal to truth); scored by the cross-check pass, so absent until a claim has been analyzed

usefulness_rationale.description: one line: which datasheet slot it fills, or which anti-pattern makes it low

analysis_status.description: verification lifecycle: pending=authored, not yet independently cross-checked; verified=cross-checked against the primary source and supported (implies cross_checked=true); sources-pending=a pass needed the primary source but could not access it (a sourcing/REQUESTS.md entry is filed); flagged=source was read but the claim is only partially supported, was downgraded, or has been superseded (see comment / supersedes)

cross_checked.description: true iff this claim has been independently verified against its cited primary source (analysis_status=verified)

last_checked.description: ISO-8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the last analysis pass over this claim

comment.description: verification caveat / uncertainty note attached by a downgrade or supersede

supersedes.description: id of a prior claim this one corrects

superseded_by.description: id of the corrected claim that supersedes this one (set on the retained, flagged original)

variant_rationale.description: Why this protein is INTENTIONALLY a variant of its UniProt accession (e.g. dCas9 = catalytically-dead SpCas9 D10A/H840A). When set, the UniProt importer KEEPS the part's variant sequence; when absent, an incidental close variant (a few SNPs) is normalized to UniProt's canonical sequence.

uniprot_features.description: Protein features imported from UniProt (domains, active/binding sites, signal peptides) -- a cached projection of the authoritative source, NOT hand-authored. Coordinates are 0-based, end-exclusive, in residue space. Regenerate with tools/import_uniprot_features.py; baked into the generated .gb so GenBank consumers get them.

type.description: GenBank feature type (protein_domain, sig_peptide, binding, misc_feature, ...)

uniprot_import.description: provenance of the uniprot_features cache (accession, UniProt release, fetch date, sequence-match status).
